title.en: Structured Output: JSON Mode, Function Calling & Pydantic
title.ta: Structured Output: JSON Mode, Function Calling & Pydantic

description.en: Force LLMs to return structured data — JSON mode, function calling, and Pydantic integration for reliable AI outputs.
description.ta: LLM-கள் structured data திருப்பி அனுப்ப கட்டாயப்படுத்துங்கள் — JSON mode, function calling, நம்பகமான AI outputs-க்கு Pydantic integration.

story_variants.en:
## Vaathiyaar's Data Factory

Vaathiyaar holds up a messy text blob and a clean JSON object. "Students, LLMs return text by default. But your Python code needs structured data — dictionaries, lists, typed objects. Today you'll learn three ways to make LLMs return EXACTLY the structure you need."

### The Problem
```
LLM says: 'The sentiment is positive with 85% confidence'
Your code needs: {'sentiment': 'positive', 'confidence': 0.85}
```

### Three Solutions

**1. JSON Mode** — Tell the API to return valid JSON
```python
response_format={'type': 'json_object'}
```

**2. Function Calling** — Define a schema, LLM fills it
```python
tools=[{'type': 'function', 'function': {...}}]
```

**3. Pydantic + Instructor** — Type-safe structured output
```python
class Review(BaseModel):
    sentiment: str
    confidence: float
```

> **Key Insight:** Structured output is what separates toy demos from production apps. If your code can't parse the LLM's response reliably, your app will break.
story_variants.ta:
## வாத்தியாரின் Data Factory

வாத்தியார் ஒரு குழப்பமான text blob-ஐயும் சுத்தமான JSON object-ஐயும் உயர்த்திக் காட்டுகிறார். "மாணவர்களே, LLM-கள் இயல்பாக text-ஐ திருப்பி அனுப்பும். ஆனால் உங்கள் Python code-க்கு structured data தேவை."

explanation.en: Import Pydantic for type-safe data models
JSON module to parse structured responses
Use response_format to force JSON output
The simplest way to get JSON — guaranteed valid JSON
Make the API call with JSON mode enabled
Most modern models support JSON mode
This forces the model to return valid JSON — no markdown, no extra text
You must mention JSON in the prompt for this to work
Describe the JSON schema you want in the system prompt
Parse the JSON response into a Python dict
json.loads() converts the JSON string to a Python dictionary
Access structured data with dictionary keys — no regex needed!

animation_sequence.left_content:
The sentiment of this text is positive.
I'd estimate about 85% confidence.
Key words: love, Python, course

# Parsing this requires regex,
# string splitting, and prayer
animation_sequence.right_content:
{
  "sentiment": "positive",
  "confidence": 0.92,
  "keywords": ["love", "Python", "course"]
}

# Clean, parseable, reliable
result = json.loads(response)
print(result['sentiment'])  # 'positive'

output: >>> # Now use it in your code!

animation_sequence.code:
from openai import OpenAI
import json
client = OpenAI()

response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model='gpt-4o-mini',
    messages=[{'role':'user',
        'content':'Extract name, age, skills: "Alice is 30, knows Python and SQL"'}],
    response_format={'type':'json_object'}
)

data = json.loads(response.choices[0].message.content)
print(f'Name: {data["name"]}')
print(f'Skills: {data["skills"]}')

executionSteps.explanation: Tell AI exactly what fields to extract
json.loads safely parses response to dict
AI extracts structured data from natural language

instruction.en: Create a Pydantic model called BookReview with fields: title (str), rating (int, 1-5), pros (list of str), cons (list of str), and recommended (bool). Write an API call using JSON mode that analyzes a book review and returns data matching this model. Parse the response into your Pydantic model.
instruction.ta: BookReview என்ற Pydantic model உருவாக்குங்கள்: title (str), rating (int, 1-5), pros (list of str), cons (list of str), recommended (bool). JSON mode பயன்படுத்தி book review-ஐ பகுப்பாய்வு செய்யும் API call எழுதுங்கள்.

practice_challenges.starter_code:
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from openai import OpenAI
import json

class BookReview(BaseModel):
    title: str
    rating: int = Field(ge=1, le=5)
    pros: list[str]
    cons: list[str]
    recommended: bool

client = OpenAI()

review_text = '''Automate the Boring Stuff with Python is excellent for 
beginners. Clear explanations and practical projects. However, it 
doesn't cover advanced topics and some examples are outdated.'''

# Make API call with JSON mode
# Parse response into BookReview model
# Print the structured review
practice_challenges.expected_output:
title='Automate the Boring Stuff with Python'
rating=4
pros=['Clear explanations', 'Practical projects']
cons=['No advanced topics', 'Some outdated examples']
recommended=True

en: Use response_format={'type': 'json_object'} in the API call
Describe the exact JSON schema in your system prompt
ta: system prompt-ல் சரியான JSON schema-ஐ விவரிக்கவும்

en: Forces the model to return valid JSON only
ta: மாதிரி valid JSON-ஐ மட்டுமே திருப்பி அனுப்ப கட்டாயப்படுத்துகிறது

q.en: Why is Pydantic useful for LLM output parsing?
q.ta: LLM output parsing-க்கு Pydantic ஏன் பயனுள்ளது?

en: It provides type validation, ensuring the LLM output matches your schema
It's required by the OpenAI API
ta: LLM output உங்கள் schema-வுடன் பொருந்துவதை உறுதி செய்யும் type validation வழங்குகிறது

q.en: What happens if you use JSON mode but don't mention JSON in the prompt?
q.ta: JSON mode பயன்படுத்தி prompt-ல் JSON குறிப்பிடாவிட்டால் என்ன நடக்கும்?

en: The API may return an error or unreliable output
Nothing — JSON mode overrides the prompt
ta: API error அல்லது நம்பகமற்ற output திருப்பி அனுப்பலாம்
ஒன்றும் இல்லை — JSON mode prompt-ஐ override செய்கிறது
